reasoning: The 30-day windows for the resolution dates are Dec 26 - Jan 24 and Jan 18 - Feb 16. The reference value is 8.583, so an outcome of more than 10 times this requires at least 86 fatalities in a 30-day period. Thailand experienced a severe border conflict with Cambodia from Dec 7 to Dec 26, 2025, resulting in roughly 70 fatalities on the Thai side. However, a ceasefire was implemented on Dec 27 and has largely held through January 18 (the forecast date). Consequently, the first 30-day window contains virtually no days of heavy fighting, meaning fatalities will be close to the baseline average of ~8.5 per month, missing the 86 threshold by a wide margin. For the second date, hitting 86 fatalities would require the ceasefire to completely collapse and for the ensuing war to be even deadlier within a 30-day span than the intense three-week December war. While possible, diplomatic efforts suggest de-escalation is more likely, keeping probabilities quite low.
